amendments.normalized_body: The `render_start_journal_details` and `render_completion_journal_details` functions now allocate owned Strings for every field (`.to_owned()`, `.map(str::to_owned)`) on every call, whereas the prior `&'a str` / borrowed payload approach was zero-copy for serialization. This is functionally correct and the allocation cost is negligible for the current usage pattern, but the API could retain a borrowing render path (e.g. a `StartJournalDetails::from_parts` constructor that borrows into a `Cow` or just accepts owned values at the call site) to avoid the regression for any future hot-path usage. This is a low-priority observation, not a blocking issue.

`update_task_run` still accepts the wrong finalized runless attempt when the caller later supplies a `run_id`. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:3013-3017`, the no-exact-match path falls back to `unique_terminal_replay_match`, and that helper (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2756-2788`) never checks `started_at` for runless terminal rows. A replay of a legacy finalized attempt with the wrong `started_at` will therefore backfill `run_id` onto that terminal row instead of rejecting it, even though Amendment 2 required `started_at` to disambiguate runless-to-named backfills. Add the same `started_at` guard for terminal fallback that you added for open matches, and cover it with a regression test.

The `plan_hash` auto-population fix is still incomplete for repair/replay flows. The snapshot hash is only consulted when appending a brand-new start row (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2932-2943`), but both existing-row branches explicitly skip snapshot backfill (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2896-2907` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:3215-3237`). That means a legacy or partial-write `TaskRunEntry` with `plan_hash: None` will stay unlabeled forever on start replay or completion replay, even when the milestone snapshot already held the canonical plan hash for that attempt. The accepted Issue 3 fix called out `update_task_run` as well, so the repair paths still need a safe snapshot-based backfill rule and tests for existing-row recovery.

The journal-detail drift fix is not complete yet. `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` are now shared model structs (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:353-394`), but the adapter still hand-merges only the current optional fields in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2284-2305` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2322-2354`. If a new field is added to the shared structs later, the code will still compile and the new round-trip tests will still pass, but `append_event_if_missing` will silently drop that field during journal repair because `merge` never copies it. Add a merge-coverage test or refactor the merge logic so future field additions fail loudly instead of truncating repaired events.

final_accepted_amendments.normalized_body: `update_task_run` still accepts the wrong finalized runless attempt when the caller later supplies a `run_id`. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:3013-3017`, the no-exact-match path falls back to `unique_terminal_replay_match`, and that helper (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2756-2788`) never checks `started_at` for runless terminal rows. A replay of a legacy finalized attempt with the wrong `started_at` will therefore backfill `run_id` onto that terminal row instead of rejecting it, even though Amendment 2 required `started_at` to disambiguate runless-to-named backfills. Add the same `started_at` guard for terminal fallback that you added for open matches, and cover it with a regression test.

The journal-detail drift fix is not complete yet. `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` are now shared model structs (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:353-394`), but the adapter still hand-merges only the current optional fields in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2284-2305` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2322-2354`. If a new field is added to the shared structs later, the code will still compile and the new round-trip tests will still pass, but `append_event_if_missing` will silently drop that field during journal repair because `merge` never copies it. Add a merge-coverage test or refactor the merge logic so future field additions fail loudly instead of truncating repaired events.

payload.summary: Final review accepted 2 amendment(s); restart required.
